revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[az:Hiko məbədi]][[ja:飛行神社]][[simple:Hiko Shrine]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = File:Hiko-Jinja Honden 01.jpg | caption = image of its Haiden demonstrating the unique Greek inspired architecture | deity = {{ill|Nigihayahi|qid=Q10949054|1=Nigihayahi}} | consecration_year = 1915 (Taishō 4) |Shrine |name=Hikō Shrine |location=44 Doi, Yawata City, Kyoto Prefecture |Latitude=34.8837778 |Longitude=135.7030000 |type= |Festivals=April 29 |Architecture={{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Map=Japan Kyoto |prefecture={{ill|Yawata|qid=Q260923|1=Yawata}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.883778|N|135.703|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |founded_by={{ill|Chūhachi Ninomiya|qid=Q3655885|1=Chūhachi Ninomiya}} }} '''Hikō Shrine''' (飛行神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yawata|en|Yawata|qid=Q260923|lt=Yawata City|1=Yawata}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. The shrine enshrines the deity of aviation, {{ill|Nigihayahi|fr|lt=Nigihayahi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}, and commemorates [[:en:Plane_crash|plane crash]] victims. It features a Greek-style Haiden and was founded by {{ill|Chūhachi Ninomiya|en|Chūhachi Ninomiya|qid=Q3655885|lt=Chūhachi Ninomiya|1=Chūhachi Ninomiya}}, the pioneer of aviation principles in Japan.<ref name=":1" />
It was founded by {{ill|Chūhachi Ninomiya|en|Chūhachi Ninomiya|qid=Q3655885|lt=Chūhachi Ninomiya|1=Chūhachi Ninomiya}} on his own land<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|title=Hiko Shrine {{!}} Sightseeing Spots|url=https://www.kyototourism.org/en/sightseeing/427/|access-date=2023-05-19|website=Another Kyoto Official Travel Guide|language=en-US}}</ref> who was one of the first people in Japan to make airplanes.
It has three altars<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Yawata Story|url=http://www.city.yawata.kyoto.jp/yawata-story/|access-date=2023-05-19|website=Yawata Story|language=en}}</ref>
# The main altar, where [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi no Mikoto]], the sky deity, is worshipped.<ref name=":0" /> # The right altar, consecration_year to the spirits of those who perished in aviation accidents and to the trailblazers of aviation.<ref name=":0" /> # The left altar, which honors famous people from Japan's pharmaceutical industry.<ref name=":0" />
The building was built again in a Greek style in 1989 to celebrate the 100 year anniversary of {{ill|Chūhachi Ninomiya|en|Chūhachi Ninomiya|qid=Q3655885|lt=Chūhachi Ninomiya|1=Chūhachi Ninomiya}}'s flight discoveries.
[[File:飛行神社 鳥居.jpg|thumb|200x200px|Stainless steel torii gate]] [[File:飛行神社 本殿.jpg|thumb|Honden with a triple-connected Nagare-zukuri structure]]
== Overview and History == Chūhachi Ninomiya successfully conducted Japan's first powered model flight in 1891 (Meiji 24). However, upon learning of the Wright brothers' successful manned flight, he abandoned aircraft development. Troubled by frequent aviation accidents, Ninomiya resolved to memorialize the victims, considering it his responsibility as a pioneer. He used his own funds to establish the shrine in 1915 (Taishō 4).
The shrine serves as a type of {{ill|Shōkonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkonsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shōkonsha|qid=Q11497810}}, akin to {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|qid=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}}, where specific deceased individuals are honored.
The shrine fell into disrepair after Ninomiya's death in 1936 (Shōwa 11) but was restored by his son, Kenjirō Ninomiya, in 1955 (Shōwa 30). In 1989 (Heisei 1), the shrine underwent renovations and expansions to mark the 100th anniversary of Ninomiya's aviation discovery. A Greek-style Haiden was constructed, and a stainless steel torii gate was erected, symbolizing durability and resistance to corrosion.
A museum displaying aviation-related artifacts, including the propeller of a Zero fighter recovered from Osaka Bay, was also established on the grounds<ref>Special feature on unique shrines, *Nikkei Veritas*, October 8, 2017, p.52.</ref>.
== Enshrined Deities == * Central: {{ill|Nigihayahi|fr|lt=Nigihayahi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}} * Right: Spirits of aviation accident victims and contributors to the aviation industry * Left: Medicine deity, {{ill|Konpira|ja|金毘羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}}, and White Dragon God
Nigi-hayahinomikoto is associated with the {{ill|Ama-no-iwafune|ja|天磐船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-iwafune|qq=}} (heavenly rock boat), drawing a connection to aviation. The deity was transferred from {{ill|Iwatate Shrine (Katano City)|ja|磐船神社 (交野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatate Shrine (Katano City)|qq=}} in {{ill|Katano|en|Katano|qid=Q819646|lt=Katano City|1=Katano}}, Osaka Prefecture.
The right shrine honors aviation industry contributors and all victims of aviation accidents worldwide. Domestic victims are enshrined by name, while international victims are honored collectively.
The left shrine enshrines Chūhachi Ninomiya's connection to the pharmaceutical industry and his devotion to {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|qid=Q94760|lt=Kotohira-gū|1=Kotohira-gū}}, as well as the White Dragon God, derived from a white snake that appeared on the grounds.
== Sub-Shrines == * Tokiwa Inari Shrine
== Festivals == * January 1: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * April 29: {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|Reisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|qq=}} *** Includes collective enshrinement of aviation accident victims and industry contributors. Pilots sometimes offer prayers during flights. * September 20: {{ill|Sky Day|ja|空の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky Day|qq=}}
== Access == * A 4-minute walk from {{ill|Iwashimizu-hachimangū Station|en|Iwashimizu-hachimangū Station|qid=Q1977185|lt=Iwashimizu-Hachimangū Station|1=Iwashimizu-hachimangū Station}} on the {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|qid=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|1=Keihan Electric Railway}}.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Shōka-dō|en|Shōka-dō|qid=Q11532219|lt=Shōkadō|1=Shōka-dō}} * {{ill|Hakkaku-dō|ja|八角堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkaku-dō|1=Hakkaku-dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11391750}} * {{ill|Shōbōji (Yawata City)|ja|正法寺 (八幡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōbōji (Yawata City)|1=Shōbō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11545557}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qid=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}}
== Additional Information == Another shrine, "Ninomiya Hikō Shrine," was established in 1991 at {{ill|Mannō, Kagawa|en|Mannō, Kagawa|qid=Q1350204|lt=Manno Town|1=Mannō, Kagawa}}, Kagawa Prefecture, near the Mokinoki Pass, where Ninomiya first conceived his aviation principles.
==Gallery== <gallery widths="170" heights="170"> File:Hiko-Jinja (Shrine about aviation) 1.jpg|The Torii File:Jet engine, Hiko-Jinja (Shrine about aviation) 1.jpg|A jet engine File:Hiko-Jinja (Shrine about aviation) 2.jpg|View from inside the Honden File:Hiko-Jinja (Shrine about aviation) 3.jpg|alt= File:Hiko-JInja 0011.jpg|{{ill|Chōzuya|en|Chōzuya|qid=Q1069667|lt=Chōzuya|1=Chōzuya}} File:Hiko-JInja 0020.jpg|A {{ill|Hokora|en|Hokora|qid=Q1442984|lt=Hokora|1=Hokora}} </gallery>
== Related Items == * {{ill|Timeline of aviation|en|Timeline of aviation|qid=Q1088229|lt=Timeline of aviation history|1=Timeline of aviation}} * {{ill|Aviation Shrines|ja|航空神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine (disambiguation)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 航空神社}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book |author=Hikō Shrine |year=1993 |title=Hikō Shrine and Chūhachi Ninomiya |publisher=Hikō Shrine |ref=Hikō Shrine and Ninomiya}}
*
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Hiko-Jinja (Yawata)}} * [https://hikoujinjya.wixsite.com/hikou Official website of Hikō Shrine]

If I get anywhere near implementing it, I will do so based on the actual completion tiers of this wiki rather than whether I added them when they were on simple english wikipedia or wherever--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Hiko-Jinja (Yawata)}} {{wikidata link|Q11665839}} [[Category:Aerospace museums in Japan]] [[Category:Aircraft accidents]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yawata, Kyoto]] [[Category:Burial monuments and structures in Japan]] [[Category:History of Yawata, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shokonsha]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
